summary: Branch guard's commit-prefix check fires on parent ticket close (no own code commit)
description:
checkBranchHasTodoCommit refuses to close any ticket without at least one
commit on the branch whose message contains 'todo:<id>'. For PARENT tickets,
this is wrong by design: parents have no code change of their own — the
work lives in children, whose commits use 'todo:<child-id>' prefixes.
The /todo-implement skill explicitly closes parents with just a note:
  todo close <parent-id> --note "All subtasks complete."

Caught manually this session — had to --force close parent 3d9717e2 even
though all children were properly closed and committed.

Fix: in checkBranchHasTodoCommit (or its caller in close.ts), skip the
commit-prefix check when ticket.relationships?.children is non-empty AND
every child is in a terminal state. The branch-match check should still
fire (parent close should still require being on the parent's branch).

Done contract: tests/branch-guard.test.ts gains a case showing parent
close succeeds when all children are closed even with no 'todo:<parent>'
commit on the branch. Existing --force escape stays in place for edge
cases.

resolution.test_function: parent close succeeds when all children are closed (no own commit needed)
resolution.note: checkBranchHasTodoCommit now skipped in close.ts when ticket has ≥1 child AND every child is terminal. Branch-match check unchanged. Half-baked parent closes (open children) still blocked. 7 new tests; 171 passing.
